label: excerpt_corruption
description: The excerpt itself is wrong: atoms are cut or merged wrongly, so knowledge truth is damaged.
why_this_family_matters: This is the highest danger overall because it poisons downstream trust and study.

label: taxonomy_bias
description: The current taxonomy influences excerpting or grouping where it should not.
why_this_family_matters: This is the owner's named nightmare mechanism: excerpting-bias.

label: wrong_box_forcing
description: Source material is forced into pre-existing boxes rather than letting the source determine rightful units first.
why_this_family_matters: This is how premature structure becomes corrupting rather than merely organizing.

label: inconsistent_boundaries
description: Blind or weakly disciplined processing yields unstable boundaries across similar passages.
why_this_family_matters: This is the strongest real danger on the structurelessness side.

label: silent_misclassification
description: A failure looks clean and plausible during study but is actually wrong at the knowledge layer.
why_this_family_matters: Silent errors can be built upon for years before discovery.

label: overgranulation_confusion
description: The tree splits one natural topic into too many leaves, making reconstruction of the common denominator harder.
why_this_family_matters: Within the narrower placement comparison, this is the more dangerous side.

label: undergranulation_blur
description: The tree keeps distinct topics merged together under one leaf.
why_this_family_matters: This is real damage, but often more visible and more repairable than overgranulation.

label: stage_contamination
description: Logic belonging to one pipeline stage leaks into another and changes the wrong kind of truth.
why_this_family_matters: This is the deepest comparative insight behind choosing C.

label: recoverability_failure
description: A failure becomes hard to repair because it is detected late or because repair requires rebuilding more than placement.
why_this_family_matters: The owner distinguishes visible placement failures from silent systemic corruption partly by recoverability.

label: detectability_failure
description: A failure is hard to notice before study, memorization, or trust transfer has already happened.
why_this_family_matters: Detectability is a major reason silent corruption outranks visible misplacement.
